product_summary: Open-source video upscaling and frame interpolation framework. It uses machine learning models (e.g. waifu2x, Real-ESRGAN, RIFE) to upscale low-resolution videos and generate intermediate frames, supporting multiple algorithms on multiple platforms.

Plan-first AI coding agent framework for OpenCode. An open-source extension framework that adds structured sub-agents, approval-based workflows, multi-language support, and integration with tools like LSP to the core OpenCode AI developer agent.

LLM-powered Hacker News archive explorer. An open-source CLI plugin that fetches Hacker News threads by ID and lets you request summaries or analyses of discussions, enabling reflection on historical tech conversations using large language models.
feature_list: Fetches HN posts and comments by ID

product_summary: Autonomous Excel spreadsheet agent. An open research project that combines an Excel file server with an AI agent to read, analyze, and edit spreadsheets through tools. It demonstrates how an LLM can manipulate Excel data, formulas, and charts programmatically.
feature_list: Excel file parsing and metadata extraction
SQL and Pandas tool integration (via DuckDB/Pandas)
Automated multi-step reasoning over spreadsheet data
Formula insertion and editing by AI
Chart generation and formatting by AI

product_summary: WhatsApp Online Status Monitor. An open-source tool that tracks a WhatsApp contact’s online/offline status in real-time, logs activity history, and can send notifications. It uses WhatsApp Web automation to detect when a user comes online or goes offline, illustrating messaging privacy leaks.
feature_list: No official API required (uses web automation)

product_summary: Automated web monitoring and alert system. Huginn is a popular open-source system for building agents that watch websites or data sources for certain keywords or changes and trigger actions like email alerts. It can be configured to track mentions, scrape pages, or run searches on a schedule.

Embedded SQL database engine in Rust. GlueSQL is an open-source library that provides a SQL execution engine (parser, planner, and runtime) which can be embedded into Rust applications. It supports multiple storage backends with no external dependencies and implements modern SQL features in pure Rust.
feature_list: ANSI SQL parsing and query execution
Runs on embedded and WASM environments

product_summary: Powerful LLM orchestration framework. An open-source Python library that helps build applications with multiple large language models and tools by chaining calls and logic. It supports complex prompt pipelines, branching, and model-specific prompt optimizations to achieve consistent results across providers.
feature_list: Chain multiple LLM calls and tools

product_summary: Lightweight open-source agent framework. Marvin provides a Pythonic way to create AI functions and agents with context management, tool use, and structured output. It emphasizes modular “AI function” design, allowing integration of external tools (web search, code execution) and maintaining conversation state for flexible agent behaviors.
feature_list: Define AI functions with type-safe outputs
Automatic validation of LLM outputs to schemas

product_summary: Local persistent memory module for LLMs. An open-source project that stores conversation context and long-term data on disk using embeddings, enabling an AI assistant to recall facts across sessions. It indexes user prompts and AI responses and retrieves relevant pieces via semantic search to augment the prompt, providing continuity over unlimited history.
feature_list: Local vector store for conversation history
Supports long-term memory beyond token limits
Integrates with VS Code and CLI assistants

product_summary: Visual prompt library and management extension for ComfyUI. This open-source add-on for ComfyUI provides a richer GUI to organize AI art prompts with categories, tags, and image previews. Users can save prompts, search by attributes, rate results, and quickly copy or apply prompts to their workflow, all within a dark-themed interface.
feature_list: Library of saved prompts with metadata

product_summary: Next.js RSC vulnerability scanner. An open-source CLI by Assetnote that tests web applications for the critical React Server Components remote code execution vulnerabilities (CVE-2025-55182 & CVE-2025-66478). It sends crafted payloads to detect if an app is exploitable, including bypass techniques and JSON output for CI integration.
feature_list: Scans for Next.js RSC RCE vectors

product_summary: Automated research paper to slides converter. An open-source pipeline that reads academic papers (PDFs or ArXiv IDs) and generates presentation slides. It uses LLMs to identify key sections, Summarize content, extract figures, and then compiles LaTeX beamer slides with text and images, providing a starting deck for the paper’s overview.
feature_list: Figure and graph extraction into slides
LaTeX Beamer or PPTX output options

product_summary: Generative AI movie studio in Blender. PALLAIDIUM is an open-source Blender add-on that turns scripts into storyboards and video sequences. It automates the process of creating scenes: using AI to generate images for story frames, suggesting camera angles, and assembling a sequence on Blender’s timeline, allowing human directors to refine or render the result.
feature_list: UI for editing and adjusting scenes

product_summary: Local Markdown and document search engine with LLM re-ranking. An open-source tool that indexes Markdown, PDF, and DOCX files on your device using both keyword (BM25/SPLADE) and vector embeddings. It supports hybrid search: quick full-text results which are then semantically re-ranked or answered by a local LLM, optionally with highlighted source excerpts.
feature_list: Simple web UI and API available

product_summary: Adaptive prompt programming library. Guidance is an open-source Python library from Microsoft that enables dynamic control of multiple LLMs in a single workflow. It allows you to write a template with special syntax to run parts of the prompt on different models or capture and reuse model outputs, effectively optimizing prompts per model idiosyncrasies for consistent cross-model results.

Multi-backend AI image generator suite. InvokeAI is an open-source Stable Diffusion toolkit with a browser-based UI allowing you to generate images using various models from different sources (Stable Diffusion, HuggingFace Hub, etc.). It features a dark-mode interface, customization of model selection, live preview, and workflow features like prompt queues and post-processing filters.
feature_list: Responsive web GUI with dark theme

product_summary: Large bilingual text-to-image foundation model. Qwen-Image is Alibaba’s open-source 20B-parameter diffusion model that excels at generating photo-realistic images from Chinese or English prompts. It has strong ability to render text (e.g., Chinese characters) correctly within images and produces high-quality, detailed visuals with relatively moderate VRAM requirements given its size.
feature_list: Accurate text rendering in generated images
Reference code and model weights available

product_summary: Terminal-based iMessage sender/receiver. This open-source macOS tool allows you to use iMessage from the command line or over SSH. It integrates with Apple’s Messages via AppleScript automation to list conversations, read incoming messages, and send new SMS or iMessages, all within a text-based TUI that supports keyboard navigation and quick switching between chats.
feature_list: Lists current iMessage conversations in terminal
Read messages and send replies via CLI
Support for group chats and SMS
Keyboard-driven UI (arrow keys, enter to select)
Hotkeys to start new chat or refresh

product_summary: Customizable open-source start page. Mue is a popular new-tab extension that offers beautiful backgrounds, inspirational quotes, to-do lists, and quick access links on each new browser tab. It includes multiple stunning themes, a clock with light/dark mode, custom search shortcuts, weather widgets, and syncs your configuration, all without tracking any user data.

Self-hosted download manager with web UI. pyLoad is an open-source, lightweight download queue manager that runs on a server or NAS. It supports downloading files from direct links, one-click hosters, or torrents via plugins, has a browser-based interface to add/manage downloads, handles reconnections and captcha solving, and can automate link checking and mirror fallbacks.
feature_list: Supports HTTP, FTP, and hoster links

product_summary: Interactive visualization of your Git history. This free web-based tool transforms your public coding activity into a cinematic timeline. It pulls data from your GitHub (or GitLab) profile – commits, repositories, stars – and generates a smooth animated story with slides or video, highlighting milestones like first repo, top languages, and contributions over time in an engaging narrative format.
feature_list: Timeline view of commits and repos
Charts of languages and contribution stats
Smooth slide transitions or video export
Shareable link for your code story

product_summary: Pure JavaScript HTML5 parser. Parse5 is a robust open-source HTML parser for Node.js and browsers that fully conforms to the HTML5 specification (including edge cases and quirks). It can parse HTML strings into DOM-like AST objects, enabling server-side HTML processing or rendering. It has no dependencies and handles malformed markup the same way modern browsers do, making it a reliable drop-in for HTML parsing needs.
feature_list: Supports all parsing modes (DOM, SAX, fragment)
Handles broken or legacy HTML gracefully
Used in many scraping and SSR tools

product_summary: CLI note-taking and journaling tool. Terminal Velocity is a fast, cross-platform terminal app inspired by Notational Velocity. It lets you create, search, and edit plain text Markdown notes with lightning speed. Notes are stored locally in a folder, can be tagged within content, and the interface allows incremental search on titles and content to instantly filter your notes. It opens your $EDITOR for full editing, making it a powerful console-based “second brain”.
feature_list: Blazingly fast fuzzy search through notes
Create or open notes by typing part of title

product_summary: Enhanced PostgreSQL CLI with autocompletion. pgcli is an open-source command-line client for Postgres that offers an intuitive interactive experience. It features inline SQL syntax highlighting, smart autocompletion for tables/columns (thanks to local database introspection), and persistent history. It’s keyboard-centric (vi-style editing supported) and makes typing and exploring databases faster and more pleasant than the default psql.
feature_list: Auto-completion of SQL keywords and schema names
Favorite query shortcuts and timing of queries
History and timing of past queries

product_summary: JavaScript toolkit for mobile deep links. deep-link.js is an open-source library that makes it easier to direct users from web pages to native apps. Developers can configure a link with custom data attributes for the mobile app scheme, fallback App Store/Play Store links, and a normal web URL. The library detects mobile browsers and automatically attempts to open the corresponding native app (or store, or web fallback) in a prioritized order, enabling seamless transitions from web content to apps.
feature_list: Handles iOS and Android scheme detection
Fallback to App Store/Play Store if app not installed
Final fallback to normal href link
No effect on desktop browsers (guards execution)
Supports custom URL scheme for any app

product_summary: Automated movie collection manager. Radarr is a popular open-source self-hosted tool for tracking and downloading movies. A fork of Sonarr (for TV), it lets you add films you want to “watch” – and then integrates with search indexers and download clients (NZB/torrent) to fetch them. It monitors for better quality releases (upgrade from HDTV to BluRay, etc.), supports categories/tags, notifies on downloads, and organizes files into your library folder structure automatically.
feature_list: Can upgrade quality when better available
Calendar and list views of upcoming releases

product_summary: Automated home listing notifier. Home Finder is an open-source project that scrapes real estate listing sites at regular intervals (e.g. hourly) and sends email alerts when new properties that match your criteria appear. Aimed at competitive housing markets, it supports deployment of your own script so you can adjust the check frequency and filters (location, price range, etc.), helping you act quickly on fresh listings.
feature_list: MIT licensed and easy to extend

product_summary: Self-hosted knowledge base and journal. Trilium is an open-source hierarchical note-taking application that can be installed on your server or desktop. It focuses on privacy and rich features: you can create nested journals or diaries, embed images and media, track moods or metadata via custom fields, search and tag entries, and even sync across devices. All data is stored locally or on your server under your control, with encryption options, providing a safe space for personal thoughts and logs.
feature_list: Rich text and Markdown note editing

product_summary: Local-first AI assistant chat platform. OpenAssistant is an open-source project aiming to provide a chat-based AI assistant that runs on your own hardware or preferred cloud, with connectors to various messaging platforms. It includes a web and chat UI and can be extended to integrate with services like WhatsApp or Telegram via plugins. The assistant can answer questions, follow instructions, and perform tasks without sending data to third-party APIs, focusing on user privacy and extensibility.
feature_list: Supports running on local LLM models
Extensible to multiple chat platforms via plugins
Web interface and chat API available

product_summary: Headless CAD automation for mesh-to-solid conversion. FreeCAD is an open-source 3D CAD software that can be used via Python scripting in headless mode (no GUI) to perform complex geometry operations. Using FreeCAD’s engine, you can automate conversion of STL mesh files into STEP solid models by running scripts or using its command-line interface. It leverages OpenCasCade under the hood to recreate solid geometry from mesh data, preserving dimensions and enabling further CAD operations, all in a self-hosted environment.
feature_list: Automated STL mesh import and processing
Sewing and solidification of mesh into solid
Exports to STEP, IGES, etc. formats
No GUI required (CLI or server mode)

product_summary: Real-time subreddit analytics pipeline. This open-source project streams Reddit data (posts/comments) from specified subreddits and performs live sentiment analysis and keyword aggregation. It stores data in a database and provides a dashboard to visualize trends such as sentiment over time, top discussed topics, and user engagement metrics. It demonstrates how to continuously collect and analyze Reddit content for insights without relying on the official API.
feature_list: Sentiment analysis on comments in real-time
Data pipeline with streaming and processing
Dashboard for sentiment and trend visualization

product_summary: AI agent workflow manager. SuperAGI is an open-source platform that helps orchestrate autonomous AI agents and human tasks. It provides a web UI where you can define objectives and see agents working through a task list (akin to a Kanban). Agents can use tools (code execution, web browsing, etc.) and collaborate or hand off tasks to humans via the interface. The system allows monitoring progress, intervening, and managing multiple agent tasks in parallel with transparency.
feature_list: Visual board for agent tasks and status
Supports code, web, and other tool plugins
Logging and traceability of agent reasoning
